architecture.summary: A recurrent neural network (RNN) model that encodes structured knowledge—sets of binary relations between events and attributes—using vector symbolic architecture (VSA) schemes, then stores the binarized VSA patterns as fixed points/attractors via associative memory plasticity rules. Partial cues are used to retrieve complete knowledge structures and their individual components (events, attributes) from the learned fixed points.
trained_components: associative memory plasticity rules for fixed-point storage

claims.statement: The model allows for efficient storage of knowledge structures, such that memorized structures as well as their individual building blocks (events and attributes) can be subsequently retrieved from partial retrieving cues.
Long-term memory of structured knowledge relies on a new principle of computation beyond the memory basins.
The model can be extended to store sequences of memories as single attractors.
